tasks.title: Define "Session" for Scandal Magnitude Floor
tasks.overview: The Scandals & Follower Loss proposal (Section 3, Magnitude) states: "No scandal should ever remove more than the player earned in their current session." The term "session" is undefined, and the architect cannot spec the magnitude calculation without a clear definition. This is a small but load-bearing design decision — the meaning of "session" determines what data the magnitude formula reads and how the floor is computed.

related_items.purpose: the proposal containing the magnitude floor rule; load Section 3 (Magnitude) to see the rule in context
the accepted core design; load if needed to verify consistency with idle/offline behavior

acceptance_criteria: Define what "session" means in the context of the magnitude floor rule — provide a clear, unambiguous definition (e.g., "time since the app was opened," "time since the last prestige," or another boundary)
Update Open Question #4 in the scandals proposal with the answer as a sub-bullet

tasks.title: Decompose Accepted Proposals into Technical Plan
tasks.overview: Both accepted proposals (Core Game Identity & Loop, Scandals & Follower Loss) need to be decomposed into concrete, assignable engineering tasks with explicit done-whens.

related_items.purpose: accepted proposal to decompose
accepted proposal to decompose

acceptance_criteria: Decompose both accepted proposals into discrete engineering tasks
Each task has explicit acceptance criteria an engineer can pick up and execute

tasks.title: Core Systems Architecture Spec
tasks.overview: Write the architecture spec for Click Farm's core game systems. This is the foundational technical document that all engineering tasks depend on. Must cover: data model for all entities (Player, Generator, Platform, Algorithm State, Engagement/Followers/Clout currencies), component boundaries between Python backend and Node.js frontend, interface contracts (API shape between client and server), state management approach, persistence strategy, technology decisions (seeded PRNG for algorithm shifts, calculate-on-open for offline gains). The two accepted proposals define what the game does — this spec defines how the systems that make it work are structured.

related_items.purpose: The accepted design this architecture must support — covers core loop, generators, algorithm, platforms, economy, prestige, offline rules
Stretch goal that bolts onto the core — the core data model should accommodate it without structural changes

acceptance_criteria: Architecture doc exists at architecture/core-systems.md
Data model section defines every entity with fields, types, constraints, ownership, and where it lives (client, server, or both)
Component boundaries section defines what the backend owns vs. what the frontend owns, with a one-sentence responsibility for each component
Interface contracts section defines the API shape between client and server — endpoints or message types, request/response shapes
Coupling analysis names every cross-component dependency and how it's managed
Technology decisions section covers: persistence format, seeded PRNG approach for algorithm shifts, calculate-on-open strategy for offline gains, state sync approach between client and server
Open questions section lists any unresolved items with owners
Assumptions section flags load-bearing assumptions explicitly

tasks.title: Scandal System Architecture Spec
tasks.overview: Write the architecture spec for the Scandal & Follower Loss event system (stretch goal). This system bolts onto the core game and introduces risk accumulators, scandal events, a PR Response modal state, and session snapshots. The architect review on the scandals proposal already sketched the accumulator contract — this spec formalizes it and defines the full pipeline from risk accumulation through scandal firing to resolution.

related_items.purpose: The accepted design defining triggers, magnitude, counterplay, and scaling rules
The core architecture spec that this system extends — must be consistent with core data model and state management

acceptance_criteria: Architecture doc exists at architecture/scandal-system.md
Data model section defines: risk accumulator entity (per-generator, per-platform, and global variants), scandal event entity, session snapshot entity
Risk accumulator contract is fully specified: state shape, update triggers (event-driven vs time-based), empire scaling function, algorithm modifier interaction, freeze/resume behavior for offline
Scandal pipeline is defined: accumulator threshold check → scandal type selection → magnitude calculation (with floor/ceiling rules) → PR Response state → resolution
PR Response state machine is specified: normal → scandal_active → resolving → normal, with transitions and timing
Session snapshot mechanism is defined: when snapshots are taken, what data is captured, how the magnitude floor is enforced
Interface contracts define how the scandal system communicates with the core game loop (events, state changes, UI signals)
The coarse risk_level enum (none | building | high) is formally contracted for UX consumption

tasks.title: Core Game Screen UX Spec
tasks.overview: Create the UX spec for the main game screen. The core proposal and UX review established key visual decisions: algorithm state as ambient weather metaphor, three-currency information hierarchy, generator display that scales to 8+ types, and the 'going viral' peak emotional beat. This task formalizes those decisions into an actionable spec that the engineer can implement. The main UX challenge identified in the review is mid-game information density: 3 platform follower counts + engagement + algorithm state + generator output on one screen.

related_items.purpose: Design intent, especially sections on Algorithm visualization (OQ#1 answer in UX review), going viral (OQ#5 answer), and the three-currency economy
Technical constraints on what data is available to the frontend and how it's delivered

acceptance_criteria: UX spec document defines screen layout and information hierarchy for the main game view
Algorithm state visualization is specified: weather metaphor, visual mood per state, instability indicator, shift transition animation
Currency display hierarchy is defined: Engagement (primary/always visible), Followers (secondary), Clout (prestige screen)
Generator display system is specified with a visual language that scales beyond 8 types (icon + color + a third differentiator)
Platform display and cross-posting interaction is specified
The 'going viral' moment is fully specified: counter acceleration, screen energy escalation, sound signature, 5-10 second sustained event
Click-to-post interaction is specified for early game active play
Spec addresses mid-game density problem with clear hierarchy decisions

tasks.title: Scandal UX Spec
tasks.overview: Create the UX spec for the Scandal system's visual and interaction elements. The UX review on the scandals proposal established: three-tier risk visualization (none → building via amber warmth → high via pulsing amber), PR Response as a timed modal with slider, and aftermath display with animated follower count. This task formalizes those decisions into an actionable spec. Must work with the architect's risk_level enum contract and PR Response state machine.

related_items.purpose: Design intent and UX review comments that established the visual treatment decisions
Technical contract for risk_level enum and PR Response state machine

acceptance_criteria: UX spec document defines risk indicator visual language: none (normal), building (amber warmth), high (pulsing amber at 1.5-2s cycle)
Accessibility verified: pulse animation is primary signal, color is reinforcement (passes CVD test)

PR Response modal is fully specified: scandal card layout, flavor text beat (1-2s before timer), damage bar, engagement spend slider, timer bar, information hierarchy for 6 elements under time pressure
Aftermath display is specified: explicit delta with animated count-down, platform identification
Scandal stacking suppression feedback is specified: secondary notification after primary resolves
Spec defines how scandal visuals integrate with the main game screen without disrupting core UX

tasks.title: Project Scaffolding
tasks.overview: Set up the client project structure for Click Farm per the core systems architecture spec. Vite + React + TypeScript client under ./client, with module skeleton matching the spec's component boundaries (game-loop, save, static-data, offline, ui). localStorage-based persistence. No server — server boundary is deferred per spec (resolved 2026-04-04). This task creates the skeleton that all subsequent engineering tasks build on.

related_items.purpose: Defines component boundaries, technology decisions (Vite+React+TS, localStorage, no server at launch), and project structure requirements

acceptance_criteria: Client project is initialized under ./client using Vite + React + TypeScript with strict mode enabled
Dev server runs via `npm run dev` and serves a minimal hello-world page that confirms the stack works end-to-end
Production build works via `npm run build`
Module skeleton directories exist under client/src matching the architecture spec's component boundaries: game-loop, save, static-data, offline, ui
A test runner (Vitest) is configured and `npm test` runs successfully with at least one passing smoke test
Linter (ESLint) is configured and `npm run lint` runs clean
Development environment setup is documented (how to install deps, run dev server, run tests, run build)

tasks.title: Core Data Model & State Management
tasks.overview: Implement the core data model and state management layer per the architecture spec. All game entities: Player, Generator (6 base types), Platform (3 at launch), Algorithm State, and the three currencies (Engagement, Followers, Clout). This is the foundation every other system reads from and writes to.

related_items.purpose: Defines every entity's fields, types, constraints, ownership, and persistence strategy
Design context for what each entity represents and how it's used

acceptance_criteria: All entities from the architecture spec's data model section are implemented with correct fields, types, and constraints
State can be persisted and loaded per the architecture spec's persistence strategy
Currency operations (earn, spend, check balance) work correctly with proper validation (no negative balances)
Per-platform follower counts are implemented as independent state per the design
Unit tests cover entity creation, mutation, persistence round-trip, and constraint violations

tasks.title: Game Loop: Post → Engage → Grow
tasks.overview: Implement the core game loop: click-to-post generates content, content produces Engagement based on type/platform/algorithm, Engagement converts to Followers over time. This is the heartbeat of the game. Early game is manual clicking; auto-posters come through the generator system (separate task).

related_items.purpose: Interface contracts for the game loop pipeline
Section 2 (Core Loop) defines the three beats and progression arc

acceptance_criteria: A click produces a post that generates Engagement per the formula in the architecture spec
Engagement-to-Follower conversion runs on the specified tick/interval
Conversion rate is affected by content type, platform, and algorithm state (reads from those systems but doesn't implement them — uses defaults/stubs if those tasks aren't complete)
The loop runs correctly as both active (clicking) and idle (auto-posting when generators exist)
Tests verify the full Post → Engage → Grow pipeline with known inputs and expected outputs

tasks.title: Generator System
tasks.overview: Implement the generator system: content types that auto-produce Engagement. 7 base generators (Selfies through Viral Stunts) unlocked progressively by follower count. Each generator has a base engagement rate, follower conversion rate, upgrade tracks (quality, frequency, platform optimization), and trend sensitivity that interacts with the Algorithm.

related_items.purpose: Generator entity definition, upgrade track contracts, interaction with Algorithm modifiers
Section 3 (Generators) — the generator roster, progression order, and per-generator properties

acceptance_criteria: All 7 base generators are implemented with correct base rates and properties per the architecture spec
Generators auto-produce Engagement at their specified rate when purchased/active
Upgrade tracks (quality, frequency, platform optimization) modify output correctly
Trend sensitivity interacts with Algorithm state to modify generator output (reads algorithm state, applies modifier)
Generators unlock progressively based on follower thresholds
Purchasing generators costs Engagement per the economy design
Tests cover: generator output calculation, upgrade effects, trend sensitivity modification, unlock thresholds

tasks.title: Algorithm System (Seeded PRNG)
tasks.overview: Implement the Algorithm shift system using a seeded PRNG. The Algorithm is a shifting environmental modifier that changes which content types perform best. It uses a deterministic seed to generate the full shift schedule — works offline, is reproducible for debugging, requires no server polling. The fuzzy shift window is a base interval ± a seeded random offset.

related_items.purpose: PRNG seeding strategy, shift schedule generation, algorithm state shape, modifier application contract
Section 4 (The Algorithm) — states, content type modifiers, late-game influence mechanics

acceptance_criteria: Algorithm states are implemented with named states that favor/penalize specific content types
Shift schedule is deterministically generated from a seed — same seed always produces same schedule
Fuzzy shift window works correctly: base interval ± seeded offset, no exact clock-watching possible
Algorithm state is queryable by other systems (generators, economy) to get current modifiers
Algorithm state advances correctly over elapsed time (needed for offline calculation)
Current algorithm state is exposed to the frontend per the interface contract
Tests cover: deterministic schedule generation, state transitions, modifier application, time-advancement

tasks.title: Platform System
tasks.overview: Implement the platform system: parallel progression tracks that multiply output. 3 platforms at launch, each with independent follower counts, content affinity (which generators perform best), and unlock thresholds based on total followers. Cross-posting across platforms with the right content mix creates multiplicative returns.

related_items.purpose: Platform entity definition, content affinity model, cross-posting contract
Section 5 (Platforms) — platform concept, content affinity, unlock progression

acceptance_criteria: 3 platforms are implemented with distinct content affinities per the architecture spec
Each platform maintains an independent follower count
Platform unlock thresholds work correctly based on total follower count
Content affinity modifies generator output per platform (e.g., photo platform boosts Selfies)
Cross-posting mechanics work: posting to multiple platforms applies per-platform modifiers
Platform state is exposed to the frontend per the interface contract
Tests cover: platform unlocking, affinity modifiers, cross-posting output calculation, independent follower tracking

tasks.title: Economy & Systems Integration
tasks.overview: Wire the three-currency economy through all game systems and integrate the independently-built systems (game loop, generators, algorithm, platforms) into a cohesive whole. Engagement flows from posting/generators, drains into upgrades and platform unlocks. Followers accumulate from engagement conversion, are affected by algorithm and platform. This is the integration task that makes the game playable end-to-end.

related_items.purpose: Interface contracts between all systems, currency flow definitions
Section 6 (Economy) — three currencies, faucets, drains, decision spaces

acceptance_criteria: Engagement is earned from clicks and generators, spent on generator upgrades and platform unlocks
Follower conversion works correctly with all modifiers applied: content type, platform affinity, algorithm state
All systems interact correctly: algorithm modifies generator output, generators feed engagement, engagement converts to followers per platform
Currency balances update correctly across all operations with no desync between systems
The game is playable end-to-end: click → earn engagement → buy generators → earn followers → unlock platforms → cross-post
Integration tests verify multi-system interactions with realistic play scenarios

tasks.title: Offline Calculation
tasks.overview: Implement the calculate-on-open system for offline gains. When the player returns, the game computes accumulated Engagement and Followers based on production rates at close × time elapsed. Algorithm state advances deterministically while offline (seeded PRNG makes this possible). Per the accepted design: no negative events fire while offline — pure positive accumulation only.

related_items.purpose: Calculate-on-open strategy, algorithm advancement during offline, state snapshot approach
Section 8 design note: no negative events offline, game state freezes favorably

acceptance_criteria: On app open, offline gains are calculated based on production rate at last close × elapsed time
Algorithm state advances correctly during offline period using the seeded PRNG schedule
Algorithm shifts during offline do NOT trigger negative effects — only production rate changes are applied
Offline gains are presented to the player on return (summary of what accumulated)
Edge cases handled: very long offline periods, algorithm cycling through multiple states
Tests verify offline calculation accuracy for various durations and algorithm state configurations

tasks.title: Prestige: The Rebrand
tasks.overview: Implement the prestige mechanic. Rebranding wipes followers and engagement, resets to zero, but awards Clout based on total followers at time of rebrand. Clout buys permanent meta-upgrades: faster engagement rates, new generator types, algorithm insight, platform head-starts. The classic prestige tension: rebrand now for small Clout or push further for bigger Clout with diminishing returns.

related_items.purpose: Prestige system contracts, Clout calculation formula, meta-upgrade definitions
Section 7 (Prestige: The Rebrand) — Clout scaling, meta-upgrade categories

acceptance_criteria: Rebranding resets Engagement and Followers to zero across all platforms
Clout is awarded based on total followers at time of rebrand, scaling per the architecture spec's formula
Clout persists across rebrands — it is never reset
Meta-upgrades are purchasable with Clout and apply their effects across all subsequent runs
Meta-upgrade categories implemented: faster engagement rates, algorithm insight (see shifts earlier), platform head-starts
New generator types unlocked via Clout are available in subsequent runs
Tests verify: reset completeness, Clout calculation accuracy, meta-upgrade persistence and effects

tasks.title: Core Game UI
tasks.overview: Implement the main game screen frontend per the UX spec. This is the primary interface the player interacts with: click-to-post, generator display, currency counters, platform view, algorithm weather visualization, and the 'going viral' peak moment. Must handle the mid-game information density challenge identified in the UX review.

related_items.purpose: Frontend data contracts — what data is available and how it's delivered from the backend
Core loop, generator roster, algorithm visualization intent, going viral moment
Core loop, generator roster, algorithm visualization intent, going viral moment

acceptance_criteria: Main game screen renders with correct information hierarchy per UX spec
Click-to-post interaction works with visual feedback
Currency display shows Engagement (primary), Followers (secondary) with real-time updates
Generator display scales to 8+ types with the visual language defined in the UX spec
Platform view shows per-platform follower counts and content affinity
Algorithm state is visualized as weather metaphor with mood shifts and instability indicator
Going viral moment triggers correctly: counter acceleration, screen energy, sustained 5-10s event
UI communicates with backend per the interface contracts in the architecture spec

tasks.title: Risk Accumulators & Scandal Events [stretch]
tasks.overview: Implement the scandal system's backend: risk accumulators that track player behavior and fire scandal events when thresholds are crossed. 6 scandal types with distinct triggers (Content Burnout, Platform Neglect, Hot Take Backlash, Trend Chasing, Growth Scrutiny, Fact Check). Empire scaling makes thresholds decrease as followers grow. Accumulators freeze offline and resume on return. Session snapshots enforce the magnitude floor.

related_items.purpose: Full technical spec for accumulators, triggers, magnitude, and session snapshots
Design intent, trigger table, magnitude rules, offline rule

acceptance_criteria: All 6 scandal types implemented with correct trigger conditions per the design
Risk accumulators update on relevant events (event-driven, not tick-driven — except Platform Neglect which is time-based)
Empire scaling: accumulator thresholds decrease as total followers increase
Algorithm state modifies accumulator increment rates per the architect's contract
Scandals fire when accumulator crosses threshold + fuzz, then accumulator resets
Magnitude calculation: 5-15% of affected platform followers, respecting floor (session snapshot) and ceiling (15% cap)
Scandal stacking suppression: only most severe fires when multiple trigger simultaneously
Accumulators freeze offline and resume from frozen state on return
Session snapshot taken on app foreground, used for magnitude floor enforcement
Coarse risk_level (none | building | high) exposed for UX consumption
Tests cover: each trigger type, empire scaling, magnitude bounds, offline freeze/resume, stacking suppression

tasks.title: PR Response & Scandal UI [stretch]
tasks.overview: Implement the PR Response interactive modal and all scandal-related UI. When a scandal fires: game transitions to scandal_active state, modal appears with flavor text and a timed engagement-spend slider. Player decides how much Engagement to spend on damage control (more spend = less follower loss, minimum 30-40% of original). Also implements risk indicator visuals on generators/platforms and aftermath display.

related_items.purpose: PR Response state machine spec, state transitions and timing
PR Response design: 10-15s window, engagement spend mechanic, damage reduction curve

acceptance_criteria: State machine transitions correctly: normal → scandal_active → resolving → normal
Scandal card modal displays: name, satirical flavor text, damage projection, engagement spend slider, timer bar
Timer starts after 1-2s comedic beat (flavor text visible but slider inactive)
Slider adjusts projected follower loss in real-time — spending max Engagement reduces loss to 30-40% of original
Doing nothing (timer expires with no spend) applies full scandal magnitude
Risk indicators visible on generators/platforms: none (normal), building (amber warmth), high (pulsing amber 1.5-2s cycle)
Aftermath display shows explicit delta with animated follower count decrease
Stacking suppression feedback: secondary notification after primary scandal resolves
Tests cover: state machine transitions, slider damage reduction calculation, timer behavior, risk indicator thresholds

tasks.title: Define Clout-to-Follower Scaling Curve for Rebrand
tasks.overview: The architecture supports any monotonically increasing function mapping lifetime followers → Clout awarded at rebrand. The specific curve is a game-design decision that determines prestige pacing (how long a first rebrand feels worthwhile, how quickly Clout scales on later runs, and when diminishing returns kick in). Without it, the engineer implementing task 28 (Prestige: The Rebrand) has no formula to implement and will either stall or silently pick a curve that sets the prestige tempo by accident.

related_items.purpose: Open Question #1 — architect has deferred this formula to DESIGN. Architecture supports any monotonically increasing function.
Section 7 (Prestige: The Rebrand) — design intent for Clout scaling and meta-upgrade pacing

acceptance_criteria: Provide a concrete curve (formula or table) mapping lifetime_followers → clout_awarded
Curve is monotonically increasing
Rationale explains the intended pacing: how many followers feels like a 'good first rebrand', and what the curve's shape communicates about diminishing returns
Answer written back into architecture/core-systems.md Open Question #1 or linked from it
Blocks task 28 (Prestige: The Rebrand) — result must be available before that task is built

tasks.title: Define Level Multiplier Curve for Engagement Rate Formula
tasks.overview: The engagement rate formula in core-systems.md includes a `level_multiplier(level)` term applied to generator upgrades. The architecture supports any curve (linear, polynomial, exponential) but the specific shape is a balance/pacing decision. It directly affects how upgrade purchases feel and how quickly generator output scales — core-loop concerns that belong to the game designer. Without it, engineer tasks 22 (Game Loop), 23 (Generator System), and 26 (Economy) have no concrete multiplier to apply.

related_items.purpose: Open Question #2 — architect has deferred this curve to DESIGN
Design intent for generator upgrade tracks and progression pacing

acceptance_criteria: Provide a concrete curve (formula or table) mapping upgrade_level → output_multiplier
Curve shape is named (linear / polynomial / exponential) and justified against intended pacing
Rationale explains what the player should feel at early, mid, and late upgrade levels
Answer written back into architecture/core-systems.md Open Question #2 or linked from it
Blocks tasks 22, 23, 26 — result must be available before those tasks are built

tasks.title: Prestige / Rebrand Screen Spec
tasks.overview: The Prestige/Rebrand screen is where the player spends Clout to reset their run and carry bonuses forward. It is the only surface on which Clout is displayed (per core-game-screen.md §5.3). It needs a full UX spec covering layout, information hierarchy, the rebrand confirmation flow, Clout display, and the emotional arc of the prestige moment.

related_items.purpose: Defines the prestige/rebrand mechanic — Clout currency, what resets, what carries forward
§5.3 establishes that Clout is not shown on the main screen — prestige screen is its home. Also defines the rebrand button affordance and Clout preview tooltip.
Data contracts for Clout, run state, and what persists across resets

Accessibility requirements met per role standards

tasks.title: Mobile Layout Pass
tasks.overview: The core game screen spec is desktop-first (1280×800). §14 sketches a mobile layout but explicitly defers it. This task produces the full mobile UX spec: thumb-reach-optimized post zone at bottom, vertical scroll generators, horizontal platform strip, algorithm mood in background. Must also resolve the open question of whether the algorithm state name + mood can be combined into a single top-strip element on mobile.

related_items.purpose: §14 contains the rough sketch this task must formalize into a full spec. All sections of the desktop spec have mobile equivalents to specify or explicitly defer.

Post zone placement and size resolved for thumb reach
Generator list scroll behavior specified

tasks.title: Settings Screen Spec
tasks.overview: The core game screen spec references a Settings screen for Reduce Motion, audio toggles, and save management but explicitly defers it. This task produces that spec. It is a utility screen — the emotional bar is low, but the Reduce Motion toggle has accessibility implications that must be fully specified.

related_items.purpose: §12 defines what Reduce Motion must affect: disables particle bursts, replaces background drift with static gradients, replaces viral zoom pulse with static glow. Tick-rate drama is preserved.

tasks.title: Purchase Feedback & Rate Visibility
tasks.overview:
Every button press in the game — buying generators, purchasing upgrades — feels imperceptible. The player can't tell if anything happened.

This has been verified by directly observing the running game at localhost:5173. The economy is working correctly — one selfies generator produces ~6.8 engagement/sec passively, which is meaningful. But the UI is completely silent: no rate indicator, no button feedback, no animation on numbers, no indication that a generator is running after purchase.

The root cause is a visibility problem: the player watches a raw total accumulate with no rate surfaced, so they can never perceive that their rate changed. The numbers are fine. This is a pure UX problem.

Specific gaps observed:
- No rate indicator (e.g. +6.8/sec) anywhere in the UI
- No visual feedback on Buy or Post Content button press
- Stat numbers silently increment with no tick animation
- Generator row looks identical before and after purchase — nothing signals it is running

related_items.purpose: Core game feel target — what the player experience is supposed to be

acceptance_criteria: Spec where and how the current engagement rate (+X/sec) is displayed — it must be prominent enough to read at a glance
Spec how the rate display reacts at the moment of purchase — the player must be able to see the delta without reading a number carefully (e.g. the rate jumps, flashes, or animates)
Spec button feedback on Buy and Post Content — press state, confirmation flash, or equivalent
Spec how a generator row communicates 'this is actively running' after purchase
The target feeling at purchase is: 'Something just changed. I did that. I can see it working.'

open_questions.question: If the UX treatment doesn't resolve the feel problem, the numbers themselves may need tuning — game-designer should revisit the upgrade delta curve after this ships.

tasks.title: Review: Clout Upgrade Menu & Costs

tasks.title: Review: Generator Balance & Algorithm States
tasks.overview:
The game designer has formalised the provisional generator balance values and algorithm state definitions. The engineer needs to review this proposal and flag anything that doesn't match actual runtime behaviour or that would be impractical to implement as specified.

Key things to verify:
- Generator rates, trend sensitivities, and costs match what's currently in static-data/index.ts (or flag discrepancies)
- The clout upgrade open question is noted: once clout-upgrade-menu.md is accepted, static-data must be updated to match that proposal's values (replacing the current placeholders)
- Algorithm state characters match how they feel in practice
- Nothing in the proposal contradicts a contract defined in core-systems.md

tasks.title: Viral burst event signal — engine contract + trigger
tasks.overview:
The Core Game UI (task #29) implements the visual choreography for the 'going viral' peak moment per UX spec §9 — counter acceleration, screen energy, sustained 5–10s cascade. The UX spec §9.1 explicitly states: 'Viral triggers are determined by the game loop, not this spec. The UX assumes the engine signals a viral burst event with source_generator_id, source_platform_id, duration_ms (5000–10000), magnitude (total bonus engagement).' No such signal exists in the current game loop. The UI has a dormant viralEvent hook ready to be driven — this task decides what fires it and how.

Architect needs to answer: (1) what in the simulation triggers a viral burst? (e.g., follower-rate spike threshold, algorithm state × generator combo, random-chance-on-post gated by state), (2) where in the tick pipeline does it emit, (3) how is the signal surfaced to the UI — is it persisted state on GameState, a transient event queue on the driver, or a one-shot callback? Game-designer owns the frequency target (UX spec §9.5 notes 15–40 minutes between virals per active play session; open question owned by game-designer).

related_items.purpose: §9 defines the visual contract the engine signal must satisfy (fields: source_generator_id, source_platform_id, duration_ms, magnitude)
Current game loop and tick contract — the viral event mechanism must fit within the existing state→state pipeline or extend it cleanly
§4 establishes viral moments as the game's peak emotional beat; Q5 resolution describes the UX cascade
Where tick pipeline lives — the emission point will go here
The driver is the UI's contact point; event surfacing from tick→UI passes through here

acceptance_criteria: Architect proposal defines trigger condition(s) for a viral burst — what simulation state fires it
Architect proposal defines the signal transport: persisted on GameState vs transient driver event vs other
Architect proposal defines the payload shape matching UX spec §9.1 (source_generator_id, source_platform_id, duration_ms, magnitude)
Architect proposal defines engagement application — is magnitude applied instantaneously or distributed across duration_ms
Game-designer signs off on trigger frequency target (default 15–40 min between virals per UX §9.5)
Document notes what changes in tick() / driver.ts / types.ts to implement

open_questions.question: What gameplay condition should trigger a viral burst? (rate spike, algorithm×generator combo, seeded rare-event, player action?)
Target frequency — is 15–40 min of active play between virals the right tuning?
Does viral magnitude apply all at once or amortized over duration_ms? Tick-rate drama (UX §9.3) suggests amortized so the counter visibly accelerates.

tasks.title: Prestige: Clout Shop modal & Rebrand Ceremony
tasks.overview:
The core game screen (task #29) is live but players currently have no way to purchase Clout upgrades — the spec intentionally keeps Clout off the core screen (§5.3), so Clout lives on the prestige surface. This task implements the two prestige surfaces defined in the UX spec: (1) the persistent Clout Shop modal accessible during a run via the Upgrades affordance, and (2) the theatrical 4-phase Rebrand Ceremony (Threshold → Eulogy → Commit → Dissolution→Rebirth, 6–10s). The underlying simulation — calculateRebrand, applyRebrand, purchaseCloutUpgrade, the clout_upgrades map — is already built (task #28). This task wires the UI to it.

Entry points to add to the core game screen: an 'Upgrades' button that opens the shop modal, and a Rebrand affordance that replaces the minimal corner button already in place. The shop modal must not pause the running game (core-game-screen.md §7.4 — the ticker keeps moving). Post-rebrand re-entry is silent (§1 / §6) — no welcome modal.

related_items.purpose: Full UX spec for both surfaces: Clout Shop modal + Rebrand Ceremony choreography
§5.3 defines Clout visibility rules; the corner Rebrand affordance lives here
Upgrade roster, costs, and effect shapes — the shop's content
purchaseCloutUpgrade, calculateRebrand, applyRebrand, cloutForRebrand, cloutUpgradeCost — all simulation APIs already exist and are tested
Host screen — add the Upgrades entry point and replace the minimal rebrand corner button with the full affordance
Already exposes rebrand() and buyCloutUpgrade(id) — no new driver API needed

acceptance_criteria: Clout Shop modal renders with the upgrade roster per ux/prestige-rebrand-screen.md §2–§4, grouped and ordered as specified
Upgrades show current level, next cost, max level, and effect delta; disabled state is perceptible when player can't afford or is at max
Purchasing an upgrade spends Clout, updates the upgrade's level, and reflects in per-upgrade state with the spec's feedback motion
Game loop continues ticking behind the modal (not paused)
Rebrand Ceremony plays the 4 phases (Threshold → Eulogy → Commit → Dissolution→Rebirth) per §5, honoring the skip rule (Eulogy skippable after first rebrand)
Commit tap is irreversible with no second confirmation (§7 / spec decision #7)
Post-rebrand: main screen returns in cleared state, no welcome modal (§6)

Core game screen's minimal corner Rebrand button is replaced by the spec's affordance, with Clout balance visible on the Upgrades button and Clout-on-rebrand preview on the Rebrand affordance (§5.3 extension)

open_questions.question: Does the ceremony need sound/audio to hit the emotional arc, and should it block on audio asset availability?

tasks.title: Generator upgrade drawer
tasks.overview: The core game screen currently exposes generator level-ups via a terse inline 'Lvl ↑' button with cost in a tooltip. The UX spec calls for a proper drawer that opens when the player taps the upgrade affordance — showing the current level, next 3 levels, per-level costs, and rate deltas. This is the surface the player uses to commit to level upgrades thoughtfully rather than reflexively. Replaces the inline Lvl ↑ affordance on generator rows.

tasks.title: Purchase feedback & rate visibility sharpening
tasks.overview: The core game screen is functional but a specific feel gap exists: purchases feel imperceptible, rate is under-surfaced, generators don't clearly communicate 'I'm running'. The Purchase Feedback & Rate Visibility UX spec sharpens these patterns as extensions to the existing core screen — it does not replace core-game-screen.md, it intensifies it. Key decisions in the spec: promote rate to full P0 weight alongside the count, add per-generator 'running' state indicators, and give purchase moments stronger feedback (visual delta on the rate, pulse choreography, etc).

tasks.title: Settings screen modal
tasks.overview: A low-emotional-bar utility surface: Reduce Motion toggle, audio toggles, save management (export/import/reset). Reduce Motion has real accessibility weight and must propagate into the game's motion language — viral cascade, algorithm background drift, instability intensification, shift transitions, click feedback, and ceremony all respect it. Settings is a modal layered over the running game, matching the Clout Shop surface grammar.

tasks.title: Fix affinity chip glow animation selector
tasks.overview:
The affinity chip glow animation on platform cards is broken due to a CSS selector mismatch. The animation is designed and implemented correctly but never fires.

The CSS selector is `.affinity-chip .glow` (descendant combinator) but the React code applies the `glow` class to the `.affinity-chip` element itself, not a child element. The selector should be `.affinity-chip.glow` (compound selector, no space).

File: `client/src/ui/GameScreen.css`
Current: `.affinity-chip .glow { animation: affinity-glow 1.5s ease-in-out infinite; }`
Fix: `.affinity-chip.glow { animation: affinity-glow 1.5s ease-in-out infinite; }`

The animation keyframes and React logic are correct — this is a one-character fix.

related_items.purpose: Line with broken .affinity-chip .glow selector
React code applying the glow class to the chip element

acceptance_criteria: CSS selector changed from `.affinity-chip .glow` to `.affinity-chip.glow`
Affinity chip glow animation fires visibly in-browser on the heaviest-contributing generator chip for each platform card

tasks.title: Viral burst engine — trigger, signal transport, magnitude
tasks.overview:
Implements the game-loop and driver side of the viral burst event per the accepted signal spec. The UI choreography is a separate task blocked on this one.

Concretely:
- Add `ViralBurstState`, `ActiveViralEvent`, `ViralBurstPayload` to `types.ts`; extend `GameState` and `StaticData`
- Add tick pipeline steps 10–12 to `game-loop/index.ts` (cooldown counter, probability gates, algorithm affinity boost, active burst amortization). Export trigger logic as a testable pure helper with injectable PRNG (`() => number`, defaulting to `Math.random()`)
- Add `onViralBurst(listener)` to `GameDriver` interface and `createDriver`. Detection: capture `state.viralBurst.active` before calling `tick()`, check null→non-null transition on the result, fire listeners synchronously before calling `applyState()` (see engineer review note in signal spec)
- Add `viralBurst` section to `static-data/` with tunable starting values per spec §4
- Save migration: bump `CURRENT_VERSION` 1→2, implement `migrateV1toV2` in save chain injecting `{ active_ticks_since_last: 0, active: null }` as default. Clear `active` in migration, not in `load()` directly

related_items.purpose: Full spec — trigger model, state shape, tick pipeline, driver contract, static data
Add ViralBurstState, ActiveViralEvent, ViralBurstPayload; extend GameState and StaticData
Add steps 10–12 to tick(); export trigger helper
Add onViralBurst to GameDriver interface and createDriver
Bump version, add migrateV1toV2, clear active on load via migration
Add viralBurst tuning section

acceptance_criteria: ViralBurstState, ActiveViralEvent, ViralBurstPayload types exist in types.ts; GameState.viralBurst field present
tick() correctly advances active_ticks_since_last each tick and evaluates all three trigger gates (cooldown, probability, algorithm affinity) when no viral is active
When a viral fires, ActiveViralEvent is constructed with correct source_generator_id, source_platform_id, duration_ms (random 5000–10000), and magnitude per the boost formula
During an active viral, bonus_rate_per_ms is added to engagement each tick on top of normal production
Viral clears correctly when now >= start_time + duration_ms
onViralBurst fires once per event, synchronously before notify(), with the correct ViralBurstPayload
Trigger helper is exported and unit-tested: each gate can be exercised deterministically via injectable PRNG
Save version bumped to 2; migrateV1toV2 injects viralBurst defaults; active is never persisted across saves
All existing tests pass

open_questions.question: OQ2 from signal spec: the `now` argument contract update to core-systems.md is still pending. Does this implementation require resolving that first, or is the existing tick signature sufficient?

tasks.title: Viral burst visual choreography
tasks.overview:
Implements the full 3-phase visual choreography for the viral burst event on the core game screen. Requires task #50 (viral burst engine) to be complete first — this task wires the UI to the `onViralBurst` driver callback and drives the visual sequence from `GameState.viralBurst.active`.

The choreography has three phases per UX spec §9.2:

Phase 1 — Build (0–1500ms): counter accelerates and shifts to viral gold; source generator row gains pulsing halo; source platform card edges illuminate; background saturation bumps +20%; rising sweep sound begins.

Phase 2 — Peak (1500–end): counter ticks at 3–5× normal rate (real — the engine is producing it); particle burst emits from source generator row drifting toward source platform card (sustained); screen-edge vignette pulses in platform-affinity color at 2s cycle; subtle ±1% zoom pulse on the screen.

Phase 3 — Decay (last 1000–1500ms): tick rate decelerates to baseline; counter color returns to baseline (400ms fade); particles dissipate; edge glow fades; summary badge 'VIRAL +N' appears near counter for 1.5s then fades.

The counter tick-rate drama is the star. All other effects frame it. Per spec §9.3: easing the tick rate across the 3 phases (accelerate → sustain → decelerate) is essential — a flat burst feels cheap.

Note: the edge vignette during viral override uses the source platform's affinity color, not the mood color. This is a state on the same vignette layer as the algorithm mood — they do not stack. See algorithm-mood-visibility.md proposal for the vignette layer architecture.

related_items.purpose: §9 full choreography spec — phases, timing, motion, sound direction
Engine contract — ViralBurstPayload shape, onViralBurst callback, GameState.viralBurst.active
Vignette layer architecture — viral burst takes over the edge vignette color during the event
Root component — viral state and onViralBurst subscription wire in here
Source generator row halo during viral
Source platform card edge illumination + particle arrival target
Counter color shift to viral gold + tick-rate drama

acceptance_criteria: Phase 1 (0–1500ms): engagement counter numerals crossfade to viral gold over 300ms; source generator row gains pulsing glow halo; source platform card border illuminates

Phase 2 (peak): particle burst emits continuously from source generator row and arrives at source platform card for the duration of the event; screen-edge vignette pulses in platform-affinity color at 2s cycle
Phase 3 (decay): counter color returns to baseline over 400ms; particles dissipate; summary badge 'VIRAL +N' (formatted) appears near the engagement counter, persists 1.5s, then fades
Counter tick-rate drama is eased: visibly accelerates in Phase 1, sustains in Phase 2, decelerates in Phase 3 — not a flat rate change
All effects respect the Reduce Motion toggle: particle burst replaced with static glow, zoom pulse disabled, counter color shift and summary badge preserved (they are informational, not decorative)
Viral state is derived from GameState.viralBurst.active — no duplicate state in the UI layer
All effects clean up correctly if the component unmounts mid-event

tasks.title: Harden save/load error reporting
tasks.overview: The driver currently swallows two error classes silently: corrupt save on load (driver:158) and quota-exceeded on save (driver:222). A transient JSON parse bug wipes the save with no trace. Add a typed error channel on the driver so the UI can surface these to the player, and distinguish 'no save exists' from 'save exists but failed to load'. Blocked on acceptance of the core-systems-architecture-review proposal (finding #3).

related_items.purpose: catch blocks at lines 158 and 222-226
load() currently returns null for both 'no save' and 'corrupt save'
hook that needs to expose the error stream to React state
proposal finding #3 — resolution details the driver channel shape

acceptance_criteria: load() returns a discriminated result — { kind: 'none' } | { kind: 'loaded', state } | { kind: 'corrupt', error } — rather than null-overloading both cases
driver exposes onSaveError(listener) channel emitting { kind: 'load_corrupt' | 'save_quota' | 'save_unknown', details?: string }
driver no longer contains bare catch {} blocks — every caught error either emits an event or logs with context
useGame hook subscribes to the error channel and exposes a saveError state field that UI components can render
tests cover: corrupt JSON triggers load_corrupt event; mocked quota-exceeded triggers save_quota event; normal operation emits nothing

tasks.title: Consolidate total_followers to a purely derived field
tasks.overview: Player.total_followers is labelled 'derived field' but is written via two paths inside the same tick: an incremental update at game-loop/index.ts:371 and an immediate syncTotalFollowers() call at line 377. The incremental write is redundant and creates drift risk for future follower-earning code paths. Make total_followers purely derived — single source of truth is syncTotalFollowers. Blocked on acceptance of the core-systems-architecture-review proposal (finding #5).

syncTotalFollowers() + earnFollowers() also does dual-write
proposal finding #5

lifetime_followers stays incremental (it has no derivation source since it survives rebrand) — do not change its write path
